reasoning: The informal statement asks to evaluate $\int_0^{\infty} \frac{\tan^{-1}(\pi x) - \tan^{-1} x}{x} dx$. The Lean formalization encodes this as the limit $\lim_{t \to \infty} \int_0^t \frac{\arctan(\pi x) - \arctan x}{x} dx$, which is the standard definition of the improper integral $\int_0^\infty$. The integrand matches literally, and the claimed value $(\pi/2)\log\pi$ is the correct Frullani integral value. Since the integrand is absolutely integrable on $(0,\infty)$, the improper Riemann interpretation coincides with the Lebesgue interpretation, so using `Tendsto ... atTop` is faithful. The `intervalIntegral` from 0 to t with t → ∞ is the conventional encoding of $\int_0^\infty$ in Mathlib.
